steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The claim: countries with very large populations can be less densely populated than countries with much smaller populations. The example must compare two countries — one with a large population and low density, one with a smaller population and higher density.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — the geographic size of Angola (1,246,700 square kilometers) with its relatively high population of 25,022,000 inhabitants.: only one country's data is relevant to the contrast claimed. ✗
  • Choice B — Angola, which has a high population of 25,022,000 inhabitants and a relatively low density of 20.1 inhabitants/square kilometer, with São Tomé and Príncipe, which has a much lower population and a higher density.: exactly the large-population/low-density vs. small-population/high-density contrast the claim requires. ✓
  • Choice C — Mauritania, which has a low density, with Ethiopia, which has a similar density despite both countries having different geographic sizes.: the passage claims a contrast in density, not similarity. ✗
  • Choice D — the populations of both São Tomé and Príncipe and Ethiopia in 2015 with their populations in 2010.: fails to set up the required comparison. ✗
That is the correct answer.
